---
title: "Methods"
---

| Slides | Background reading | Primary literature | 
| -- | -- | -- |
| [<span class="blue-badge">Methods & Interpretation ↗</span>](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p)  | SGN Ch. 2: *The Toolbox*  | McCabe & Castel 2008 |


::: {.callout-tip}
## Learning Goals

1. Use basic neuroanatomy terms to "navigate" the brain
1. Understand the difference between spatial and temporal resolution for brain imaging studies
1. Know the names of the major methods used to study language in the brain, and identify one pro and one con of each
1. Understand the process for reading scientific papers
1. Identify one thing that brain data can tell us, and one thing it can't

:::

<!-- lobes; anterior, posterior, inferior, superior, medial, ventral -->
